behaviors.description: The generated reply references specific points from the incoming email rather than producing a generic response.
behaviors.justification: Failure depends on real LLM judgment about relevance/specificity of thread details (LLM-DEP). A stubbed model can always return a pre-baked specific-looking answer and hide real generic-output regressions (MOCK-HIDE). Rejected Workflow: behavior text targets one output property of generation ('generated reply references specific points...'), not a multi-step pipeline correctness condition.
behaviors.test_description: Use a real incoming thread with identifiable facts (names, dates, asks) and run real draft generation with the production prompt/model. Assert the draft grounds itself in thread-specific points (e.g., mentions key facts/requests) and fails if it stays generic.

behaviors.description: The factual consistency check flags claims in the draft that contradict entries in the knowledge base.
behaviors.justification: This behavior is about checker+KB module interaction (MOCK-MISS at module boundary), and can be validated with in-process wiring and a local KB fixture. No required real OS/network/subprocess boundary is stated (REAL-INFRA no). Rejected Agentic: behavior text specifies contradiction detection against KB entries, but does not require model-quality judgment; it can be validated as deterministic checker behavior.
behaviors.test_description: Wire draft parser/claim extractor, fact-check service, and local KB fixture. Provide a draft containing known contradictory claims and verify contradictions are flagged with correct claim-to-KB linkage.

behaviors.description: When the knowledge base has no relevant entries, the draft includes a disclaimer rather than fabricating facts.
behaviors.justification: Correctness depends on model output behavior under missing context (LLM-DEP): whether it abstains/disclaims vs hallucinates. Mocked/stubbed outputs would hide fabrication failures (MOCK-HIDE). Rejected Workflow: behavior text evaluates one generation outcome under a condition ('KB has no relevant entries'), not full multi-stage agent pipeline success.
behaviors.test_description: Run retrieval with an empty/no-match KB condition and invoke real draft generation. Assert the produced draft includes the required uncertainty/disclaimer language and does not introduce unsupported factual claims.

behaviors.description: The approval workflow blocks sending until the user explicitly approves — no draft is sent automatically.
behaviors.justification: Primary risk is orchestration/state handoff between draft state, approval state, and sender adapter (MOCK-MISS). This can be caught by wiring modules with a fake sender and asserting no send call before explicit approval. Rejected System: behavior text is gating semantics ('blocks sending until user explicitly approves'), not provider/network behavior, so real email API/OS infra is not required.
behaviors.test_description: Execute workflow with generated draft present but no approval event; verify sender adapter is not invoked. Then emit explicit approval event and verify exactly one send invocation occurs.

behaviors.description: The full sequence — read thread, generate draft, check facts, present for approval — works end-to-end for a real email thread.
behaviors.justification: Behavior explicitly requires end-to-end validation of the full agent pipeline, including an LLM-driven generation stage plus downstream handoffs and gating (MULTI-STEP/full pipeline). Lower levels can pass while stage-to-stage failures appear only in sequence. Rejected Agentic: text demands the whole sequence ('full sequence ... works end-to-end'), not only single-model output quality.
behaviors.test_description: Run production-like pipeline on a real email thread fixture: ingest thread, generate with real model, run fact-check against KB, and surface approval step. Assert stage outputs and transitions are correct end-to-end, including that approval is requested before any send path is available.
